meaning: n.无信用,疑惑,不名誉,耻辱vt.不信,怀疑,使丢脸
examples: His political adversaries would like to discredit him.
Agents provocateurs may seek to discredit the opposition.
Violent football fans bring discredit on the teams they support.
That David believed the best of us, that he was wrong is to our eternal discredit.
Over the weekend, the Obama administration did its best to discredit the rating agencies.
